description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body and slightly bulging sides, constructed from vertical wooden staves tightly bound by two wide, metallic hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns and subtle knots, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The top surface is flat and appears to be made from the same wood as the sides, with the staves meeting at the center. The metallic hoops are a dull gray, suggesting they are made of galvanized steel or iron, and they provide structural reinforcement to the barrel. There are no visible handles, spigots, or decorative markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with metal hoops, flat top, and rustic finish, lacking handles or markings. The surface shows natural wood grain and reinforced metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full round face of the barrel, with both metal hoops and wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the staves and the seamless connection of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to the front, with the wood texture and metal bands prominent.
description_view_3: Another angled view highlighting the barrel's bulging sides and the continuity of the wood grain.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
